danger_signals.visual_form: opposing player positioning and tackling animations
danger_signals.meaning: active threat to player possession and scoring capability

danger_signals.visual_form: scoreboard display showing higher opponent score
danger_signals.meaning: losing match state—increased pressure to score
diminishing window for recovery or goal-scoring

safety_signals.visual_form: player character highlighted/controlled on-field with ball at feet
player positioning near opponent goal zone on bird's-eye view

faction_indicators.color_identity: nation-specific team jersey colors (inferred from real-world football uniforms)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: national flag or team emblem on jersey/HUD
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: opposing selected nation rendered with different jersey color/emblem
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: stadium association with host nation (Italia '90 set in Italy)

faction_indicators.color_identity: contrasting team jersey from selected nation
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: all 24 playable nations feature distinct visual team identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: opponent bench positioning on opposite side of pitch

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: live scoreboard, player positioning on pitch, ball location, stadium environment active
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: continuous player movement, tackling attempts, ball possession transitions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: player character highlighted/controllable with ball proximity indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: responsive directional control, passing/shooting action availability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: opponent team player highlighted with ball, player team rendering defensive stance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: opponent AI executes passing patterns, attempts to advance toward player goal

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: scoreboard increments, possible celebration animation, match pause
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: ball reset to center, play resumes from kick-off

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: group stage match display indicates opponent nation and stage (implied by available 24 nations and master system official groups)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: match results contribute to advancement through tournament bracket

environmental_storytelling.example: game set in Italy (Italia '90) with visual stadium environment evoking 1990 tournament context
24 playable nations (including non-qualifiers like China, Japan) communicate global football participation scope; exclusion of Republic of Ireland indicates licensing/political constraints
FIFA World Cup official logo and mascot presence signals tournament legitimacy and real-world connection
overhead tactical view communicates strategic team positioning and field control dynamics

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: responds to input commands for movement, passing, shooting; highlighted/marked for control
advance toward player goal, execute coordinated passing sequences
position between player and goal, attempt tackles to dispossess
goal/no-goal decision triggers scoreboard update or play continuation

notes: World Cup Italia '90 is a minimal sports simulation on Genesis/Master System with limited visual complexity typical of early 1990s hardware. World-state communication relies heavily on scoreboard numerics, team jersey differentiation, and positional player rendering rather than sophisticated environmental or narrative signals. The bird's-eye view constrains ability to render detailed danger/safety visual language—safety/danger are primarily implicit in possession state and scoreline. Faction identity is communicated exclusively through national team colors and emblems. Political dimensions (nation selection, exclusion of Ireland) are meta-game rather than in-engine manifestations. Game lacks narrative or faction conflict beyond competitive match structure.
